ucs.title: [LLM] APT3 (Gothic Panda / Buckeye) accessibility-feature backdoor via Image File Execution Options debugger
ucs.description: Hunts the signature APT3 / Buckeye persistence + privilege-escalation pattern of registering cmd.exe (or another shell) as the IFEO 'Debugger' value for sethc.exe, utilman.exe, osk.exe, narrator.exe or magnify.exe — letting them invoke SYSTEM-level cmd from the Windows logon screen over RDP. Symantec (Buckeye/APT3) and FireEye (Pirpi) both documented this exact tradecraft against Hong Kong political targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Registry.registry_value_data) as cmd values(Registry.process_name) as proc values(Registry.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\*" (Registry.registry_path="*\\sethc.exe\\Debugger" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\utilman.exe\\Debugger" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\osk.exe\\Debugger" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\narrator.exe\\Debugger" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\magnify.exe\\Debugger" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\displayswitch.exe\\Debugger" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\atbroker.exe\\Debugger") by Registry.dest Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | rename dest as host | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winlogon.exe","sethc.exe","utilman.exe","osk.exe","narrator.exe","magnify.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as host] | stats values(*) as * by host | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// APT3 / Buckeye accessibility-feature backdoor — IFEO Debugger redirect of sethc/utilman/etc.
let _accessibility_bins = dynamic(["sethc.exe","utilman.exe","osk.exe","narrator.exe","magnify.exe","displayswitch.exe","atbroker.exe"]);
let _ifeo_root = @"\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Image File Execution Options\";
// 1. Registry write of an IFEO Debugger value targeting an accessibility binary
let RegHits = DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"Image File Execution Options"
    | where RegistryValueName =~ "Debugger" or RegistryValueName =~ "GlobalFlag" or RegistryValueName =~ "MonitorProcess"
    | extend HijackedBin = tolower(tostring(split(RegistryKey, "\\")[-1]))
    | where HijackedBin in (_accessibility_bins)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"            // exclude SYSTEM/machine
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("trustedinstaller.exe","msiexec.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, HijackedBin, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName,
              RegistryValueData, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName,
              InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
// 2. The post-trigger artefact — winlogon.exe / accessibility binary spawning a shell
let ProcHits = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winlogon.exe","sethc.exe","utilman.exe","osk.exe","narrator.exe","magnify.exe")
    | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","powershell_ise.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel;
union isfuzzy=true
    (RegHits | extend Signal = "IFEO debugger registered on accessibility binary"),
    (ProcHits | extend Signal = "Accessibility binary or winlogon spawned interactive shell")
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT3 accessibility-feature hijack — cross-source: registry write + post-trigger shell
let _access_bins = dynamic(["sethc.exe","utilman.exe","osk.exe","narrator.exe","magnify.exe","displayswitch.exe","atbroker.exe"]);
let RegSig = ImRegistryEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where RegistryKey has @"Image File Execution Options"
    | where RegistryValue =~ "Debugger" or RegistryValue =~ "GlobalFlag" or RegistryValue =~ "MonitorProcess"
    | extend HijackedBin = tolower(tostring(split(RegistryKey, "\\")[-1]))
    | where HijackedBin in (_access_bins)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | where ActingProcessName !in~ ("trustedinstaller.exe","msiexec.exe")
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, HijackedBin, RegistryKey, RegistryValue,
              RegistryValueData, ActorUsername, ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine
    | extend Signal = "IFEO Debugger registered on accessibility binary";
let ProcSig = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName has_any ("\\winlogon.exe","\\sethc.exe","\\utilman.exe","\\osk.exe","\\narrator.exe","\\magnify.exe")
    | where NewProcessName endswith "\\cmd.exe"
         or NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe"
         or NewProcessName endswith "\\pwsh.exe"
         or NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell_ise.exe"
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname = Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
              ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
    | extend Signal = "winlogon/accessibility binary spawned interactive shell";
union isfuzzy=true RegSig, ProcSig
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: APT3 / Buckeye Accessibility Features IFEO Debugger Hijack
id: 7c6d0f4a-2c1e-4a8d-9c3a-apt3-ifeo-001
status: experimental
description: Detects registration of a Debugger value under Image File Execution Options pointing at an accessibility binary (sethc/utilman/osk/narrator/magnify) — the signature APT3/Buckeye lock-screen-to-SYSTEM backdoor.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0022/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1546/008/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.privilege_escalation
  - attack.t1546.008
  - attack.g0022
logsource:
  category: registry_set
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_path:
    TargetObject|contains: '\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\'
    TargetObject|endswith:
      - '\\sethc.exe\\Debugger'
      - '\\utilman.exe\\Debugger'
      - '\\osk.exe\\Debugger'
      - '\\narrator.exe\\Debugger'
      - '\\magnify.exe\\Debugger'
      - '\\displayswitch.exe\\Debugger'
      - '\\atbroker.exe\\Debugger'
  filter_installers:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\\TrustedInstaller.exe'
      - '\\msiexec.exe'
  condition: selection_path and not filter_installers
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate accessibility software vendors registering app-compat shims (rare)
level: high
ucs.rationale: This is APT3/Buckeye's documented hands-on-keyboard escalation pattern (Symantec Buckeye, FireEye Pirpi). They specifically used the IFEO Debugger value on accessibility binaries so that pressing Shift x5 / Win+U at a locked RDP session yielded a SYSTEM cmd — letting them act on objectives without needing a credential dump first. Combining the registry artefact AND the post-trigger child of winlog
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] APT3 RDP-pivot lateral movement preceded by LSASS credential extraction (Pirpi/SHOTPUT chain)
ucs.description: Hunts APT3's documented post-exploitation sequence — local recon (net/ipconfig/systeminfo) + LSASS access for credential dumping (T1003.001), followed within minutes by an outbound RDP/SMB session from the same host using the same operator account to a server they had not previously authenticated to. Symantec Buckeye reports describe this exact sequence prior to deployment of Pirpi/SHOTPUT on adjacent hosts.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe") OR (Processes.process IN ("*MiniDump*","*lsass*","*comsvcs.dll*") AND Processes.process_name IN ("rundll32.exe","powershell.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","tasklist.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eventstats dc(process_name) as recon_breadth by dest user | where recon_breadth >= 4 | join type=inner dest user [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest_port=3389 OR All_Traffic.dest_port=445) All_Traffic.action!=blocked by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port All_Traffic.user _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | rename src as dest dest as remote_host | eval pivot_time=_time] | where pivot_time >= firstTime AND pivot_time <= lastTime + 1800 | table firstTime dest user recon_breadth cmd remote_host dest_port pivot_time | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
// APT3 / Gothic Panda — recon burst + LSASS access -> outbound RDP/SMB pivot on same operator account
let Window = 30m;
let ReconBins = dynamic(["net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","route.exe"]);
// 1. Hosts where one operator runs >= 4 distinct recon binaries in a 30-min window
let ReconBursts = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","explorer.exe")
    | where FileName in~ (ReconBins)
    | summarize ReconBins = make_set(FileName), ReconStart = min(Timestamp), ReconEnd = max(Timestamp)
              by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, Window)
    | where array_length(ReconBins) >= 4;
// 2. LSASS access / credential-dump indicators on the same host+user (APT3 used MiniDumpWriteDump via comsvcs.dll, procdump, custom tools)
let LsassTouches = union isfuzzy=true
    (DeviceProcessEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
        | where AccountName !endswith "$"
        | where (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDump","#24"))
             or (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass")
             or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Out-Minidump","MiniDumpWriteDump","-MemoryDumpPath"))
             or (ProcessCommandLine has "sekurlsa" or ProcessCommandLine has "lsadump")
        | project DumpTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, DumpCmd = ProcessCommandLine, DumpTool = FileName),
    (DeviceEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
        | where ActionType in ("OpenProcessApiCall","ReadProcessMemoryApiCall")
        | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
        | where AdditionalFields has "lsass"
        | project DumpTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
                  DumpCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine, DumpTool = InitiatingProcessFileName);
// 3. Outbound lateral RDP (3389) / SMB (445) from that host+user shortly after
let Pivots = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
    | where RemotePort in (3389, 445)
    | where ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | project PivotTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              RemoteIP, RemotePort, PivotProc = InitiatingProcessFileName, PivotCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
ReconBursts
| join kind=inner LsassTouches on DeviceId, AccountName
| where DumpTime between (ReconStart .. ReconEnd + 30m)
| join kind=inner Pivots on DeviceId, AccountName
| where PivotTime between (DumpTime .. DumpTime + 60m)
| project ReconStart, DumpTime, PivotTime,
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          ReconBins, DumpTool, DumpCmd,
          RemoteIP, RemotePort, PivotProc, PivotCmd
| order by PivotTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// APT3 recon -> LSASS dump -> RDP/SMB pivot chain
let Window = 30m;
let ReconBins = dynamic(["net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","netstat.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","arp.exe","route.exe"]);
let ReconBursts = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where AccountType == "User"
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where Bin in (ReconBins)
    | summarize ReconSet = make_set(Bin), ReconStart = min(TimeGenerated), ReconEnd = max(TimeGenerated)
              by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, Window)
    | where array_length(ReconSet) >= 4;
let LsassTouches = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where (NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe" and CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and CommandLine has_any ("MiniDump","#24"))
         or (NewProcessName has_any ("\\procdump.exe","\\procdump64.exe") and CommandLine has "lsass")
         or (NewProcessName endswith "\\powershell.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("Out-Minidump","MiniDumpWriteDump","sekurlsa","lsadump"))
    | project DumpTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, DumpCmd = CommandLine, DumpTool = NewProcessName;
let Pivots = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID in (4624, 4648)
    | where LogonType in (3, 10)                                  // Network or RemoteInteractive
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where ipv4_is_private(IpAddress)
    | project PivotTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              RemoteHostFromAuth = WorkstationName, IpAddress, LogonType;
ReconBursts
| join kind=inner LsassTouches on Computer, Account
| where DumpTime between (ReconStart .. ReconEnd + 30m)
| join kind=inner Pivots on Computer, Account
| where PivotTime between (DumpTime .. DumpTime + 60m)
| project ReconStart, DumpTime, PivotTime, Computer, Account, ReconSet,
          DumpTool, DumpCmd, RemoteHostFromAuth, IpAddress, LogonType
| order by PivotTime desc
ucs.rationale: FireEye (Operation Clandestine Wolf/Fox) and Symantec (Buckeye) both document APT3 operators following a tight, repeatable on-keyboard sequence: drop a Pirpi/SHOTPUT loader, run a burst of net/ipconfig/systeminfo recon, dump LSASS for credentials (they favoured custom tooling that called MiniDumpWriteDump and procdump), then immediately pivot via RDP/SMB to internal servers. The detection requires
